name: our_canonical_request
note: Bytes 0x65 0x6c are CAPABILITIES_MSG_CHECK_BYTE_1/2 ('e','l'). Next 4 bytes are the nibble-packed supported-version array (BleTransportCapabilitiesRequestMessage::SetSupportedProtocolVersion, BleLayer.cpp:135-155): even index -> low nibble of slot[index/2], odd index -> high nibble. Version 4 at index 0 -> slot0 = 0x04, remaining slots 0x00. MTU 0 = unknown/unobserved (BLEEndPoint.cpp:991-998 falls back to platform delegate when 0). Window 6.

name: expected_chip_peripheral_response
note: BleTransportCapabilitiesResponseMessage::Encode layout (BleLayer.cpp:206-223): check bytes 0x65 0x6c, selected version (1 byte), fragment size (u16 LE), window (1 byte) = 6 bytes total (kCapabilitiesResponseLength). Fragment size 244 = assumed ATT_MTU 247 minus 3-byte ATT header (BLEEndPoint.cpp:1000-1010 clamp formula), a common BLE default MTU -- not an observed value. PROVISIONAL until morning btmon capture against the Pi DUT confirms the real peripheral's negotiated MTU/fragment size.
